faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Elmont home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small irregular holes in wool clothing, carpets, or upholstery, webbing in fabric creases, silken tubes or cases on garments, and seeing small beige or gold moths flying in closets or bedrooms. You may also notice larvae (small white caterpillars) on affected fabrics. If you notice any of these signs in your Elmont home, contact us immediately for an inspection.

faqs.question: What factors affect the cost of clothes moth control in Elmont?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control pricing ($550 – $5,000) depends on the severity of the infestation, the size of your home, the number of affected rooms, and the type of treatment required. Smaller infestations in one or two closets typically cost less, while whole-home treatments involving multiple rooms, specialized heat treatments, or fumigation fall on the higher end. We provide free inspections and transparent quotes for all Elmont properties.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take to work in Elmont homes?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment typically shows immediate results with adult moths, but complete elimination requires addressing all life stages including eggs and larvae. Most infestations require 2-3 treatments over 4-8 weeks for full eradication. We'll provide a customized treatment schedule based on your specific situation and conduct follow-up inspections to ensure your Elmont home remains moth-free.

faqs.question: Can clothes moths damage more than just clothing?
faqs.answer: Yes, clothes moths feed on natural fibers including wool, silk, fur, feathers, and even leather. They can damage carpets, rugs, upholstered furniture, curtains, blankets, tapestries, and stored furs. In Elmont homes with vintage furniture or valuable textile collections, the damage can be extensive and costly. Our comprehensive treatment protects all vulnerable items throughout your home, not just your closets.
